response:
the error occurs because your .net 7 project is trying to load a winforms or wpf assembly that targets the older .net framework version instead of the modern .net core runtime.

you need to ensure that your wpf project is explicitly configured to target .net 7 or higher so that the correct assemblies are referenced and compiled for the cross platform build.

cousin projects in your solution that rely on wpf must be updated to use the new sdk style project format to avoid version mismatches during the build process.

installing the specific wpf workload for windows within your development environment ensures that the required native libraries are present when you build the application on a windows machine.

finally verify that your project file includes the correct property group settings to conditionally include wpf dependencies only when the target framework is windows to prevent the file not found exception.
